title: Exclude Soft-Deleted Objects from ERD
story: As a DataTools Pro user viewing the Entity Relationship Diagram, I want soft-deleted objects to be automatically excluded from the ERD, so that the diagram accurately reflects only the active objects in my data model.
description:
When an object is removed (soft-deleted) from the Dictionary, it continues to appear in the ERD. The SQL query that populates the ERD needs a filter condition to exclude soft-deleted items.

The root cause was that the query retrieved objects based on `user_id` rather than `team_id`, which meant the soft-delete flag was not being evaluated in the correct scope. After switching to a `team_id`-based lookup, soft-deleted objects are properly excluded from the diagram.
acceptance_criteria: Soft-deleted objects no longer appear in the ERD diagram
Objects removed from the Dictionary are immediately excluded from the ERD on next load
ERD query filters on team_id to correctly scope soft-delete checks
Existing non-deleted objects continue to render normally in the ERD
ux_changes: Soft-deleted objects no longer appear as nodes in the ERD diagram, keeping the visual representation in sync with the active data model.
middleware_changes: Updated the ERD object query to filter by team_id instead of user_id, ensuring the soft-delete flag (isdeleted) is correctly evaluated at the team scope.
implementation_notes: The initial fix was reported as complete but the issue persisted on re-test. The root cause was identified as querying objects by user_id rather than team_id. Once the query was corrected to use team_id, the soft-delete filter worked properly.
comment_summary: Developer initially reported the fix was already in place, but re-testing showed the issue persisted. After debugging, the root cause was identified as a user_id vs team_id scoping issue in the query. Fix was deployed and verified.
